model.quantization: F32 Safetensors with 637997088 parameters plus a NeMo checkpoint
model.license: OpenMDW-1.1 custom model license; explicit dependency and legal disposition required

runtime.boundary: official Python/PyTorch path; supported product integration lists Linux and NVIDIA GPU hardware

energy.notes: Runtime and license gates stopped the Apple-local benchmark.

engineering_cost.conversion_effort: An owned MLX route would need FastConformer, cache-aware streaming, RNNT decoding, locale prompting, and checkpoint conversion.
engineering_cost.update_cost: The repository would own parity with both the NVIDIA architecture and its Transformers integration.
engineering_cost.packaging_cost: The official Python/PyTorch stack is not an acceptable native product boundary; a converted model cannot ship before OpenMDW-1.1 is accepted.

reproduction.command: No benchmark command: OpenMDW-1.1 and the absence of an accepted Apple-local runtime block product evaluation.

result.blocker: The model now has both NeMo and Safetensors/Transformers paths and real cache-aware streaming, but the official supported integration remains Python/PyTorch on Linux/NVIDIA. Its OpenMDW-1.1 license also lacks the explicit ADR-0005 disposition required before redistribution or product use.
observations: The artifact must not be described as NeMo-checkpoint-only.
Transformers exposes offline and streaming RNNT inference, but that does not create a native Apple product boundary.
A community MLX model adapter cannot bypass either the runtime policy or the custom-license review.
